channel.title: Fire (Ice fork) — Updates
channel.description: Sparkle update feed for the Fire maintained fork of jordanbaird/Ice. Releases published here flow into the in-app "Check for Updates" dialog.

item.title: 0.11.13-fire.8
item.description:
fire.8 — AI can now move menu bar items

First fire build where AI assistants can actually rearrange the menu bar, not just read it.

What's new

  • Real write ops via MCP: move_item, hide_item, show_item, apply_layout now post real CGEvent drags. AI moves items between sections like you would by hand.
  • New MCPBackend.xpc service handles writes; existing MenuBarItemService.xpc stays for sourcePID lookups.
  • Lean Mover.swift (~530 lines): faithful port of upstream Ice's CGEvent drag pipeline. Same three-EventTap synchronization dance, decoupled from AppState.
  • SourcePIDCache shared across XPC services so macOS 26 Control Center reparenting resolves correctly (no more bundle IDs collapsing to com.apple.controlcenter).

From fire.7.1 (included)

  • list_layouts MCP tool — AI can ask "what layouts has the user saved?" without trial-and-error.

From fire.7 (included)

  • Multi-display support in list_items — iterates all active displays, picks the right Ice control items per display.

Full release notes on GitHub

item.title: 0.11.13-fire.8.1
item.description:
fire.8.1 — moves now work on macOS 26

Hotfix on top of fire.8. The wire plumbing shipped in fire.8 was correct, but a live smoke test through Claude Code uncovered two bugs that kept moves from actually landing on macOS 26.

Fixes

  • Real bundle IDs in list_items: SourcePIDCache was never started in MCPBackend.xpc, so every item resolved as com.apple.controlcenter on macOS 26 Control Center reparenting. Now resolved correctly.
  • Section detection works: Ice's three control items live in NSStatusBar and are NOT separate CG windows, so the section algorithm always fell through to "everything is alwaysVisible". Ice main app now publishes its section-divider X positions to its plist, and MCPBackend reads them as section boundaries.
  • Apple widgets stay Apple's: Control Center widgets (BentoBox, Clock, FaceTime, MusicRecognition, AudioVideoModule) are now identified by their stable window titles, so moves that land them next to Ice's section divider still classify correctly.
  • XPC service displays: CGGetActiveDisplayList sometimes returns zero displays from an XPC service with no graphics connection; falls back to the main display so list/move still work.

Live verified: a Claude Code session ran list_items (returned 21 real items grouped by section), then move_item bundle_id=com.electron.ollama to_section=alwaysVisible physically slid the item to the rightmost slot — confirmed by the next list_items showing ollama at pos=19.

Full release notes on GitHub

item.title: 0.11.13-fire.8.4
item.description:
fire.8.4 — AI can hide menu bar items into any section

The big one: AI assistants can now move items into the Hidden and Always-Hidden sections, not just rearrange the visible row. hide_item, show_item, move_item, and apply_layout all work across every section.

What changed

  • Ice-performs-writes bridge: moving an item into a collapsed/empty section can't be done from the MCP helper process by coordinate dragging (the section's divider is parked off-screen). Write operations are now delegated to the Ice app itself via a local file channel, so they use the exact code path Ice's own Layout editor uses — which reaches every section.
  • Off-screen dividers found correctly: control items are located via the same active-space enumeration Ice's editor uses, so an empty Hidden section is a valid drop target.

Live verified end-to-end via Claude Code: hide_item moved Ollama from the visible row into the (empty) Hidden section — it vanished from the menu bar — and show_item brought it straight back.

Local-only: writes go through a file on your Mac, never the network.

Full release notes on GitHub

item.title: 0.11.13-fire.9
item.description:
fire.9 — AI Quotas in the menu bar

Fire is a menu-bar manager for AI-heavy Macs. fire.9 adds AI Quotas: an optional menu-bar readout of how much usage you have left across Codex, Claude, Gemini, and Ollama, plus reset times.

Combined menu-bar item, e.g.:

AI Cx99 Cl81 Gm? Ol100

The dropdown breaks each provider down into session and weekly windows with reset times. A provider that can't be read shows ?.

Details

  • Off by default. Enable it in Settings → Advanced → AI Quotas.
  • Local-only. Usage is read via your local CodexBar CLI installation; quota data never leaves your Mac (not sent to Sentry or MCP).
  • One menu-bar item with a stable position; refreshes every 5 minutes, or on demand from the dropdown.
  • Needs the CodexBar CLI installed (auto-detected, or set a custom path in settings).

Also includes the fire.8.x menu-bar write bridge: AI assistants can move and hide items across every section via MCP.

Full release notes on GitHub

item.title: 0.11.13-fire.9.2
item.description:
fire.9.2 — AI Quotas adds Antigravity

AI Quotas now tracks Antigravity (Google's Gemini-backed coding tool) alongside Codex, Claude, Gemini, and Ollama. The combined item reads e.g.:

AI Cx99 Cl73 Gm? Ag100 Ol100

The dropdown breaks Antigravity down by model (Gemini 2.5/3 Pro, Flash, etc.).

  • New providers added in an update now appear automatically (provider selection persists the disabled set, so the default stays "all on").
  • Still off by default, still local-only.

Full release notes on GitHub

item.title: 0.11.13-fire.9.4
item.description:
fire.9.4 — AI Quotas now actually shows in the menu bar

Fixes a real architectural bug: the AI Quotas readout could end up invisible (parked off-screen) because Ice is a menu-bar manager and was treating its own readout like a third-party item to hide.

Rebuilt the AI Quotas item as a first-class, always-visible Ice element (same mechanism Ice uses to keep its own icon visible). It now sits reliably in the visible part of the menu bar: AI Cx99 Cl73 Gm? Ag100 Ol100.

Still off by default, still local-only.

Full release notes on GitHub

item.title: 0.11.13-fire.9.5
item.description:
fire.9.5 — AI Quotas gets brand icons

The AI Quotas readout now shows each provider's logo next to its weekly usage, instead of cryptic two-letter codes:

  [Codex] 22%  [Claude] 11%  [Antigravity] 0%  [Ollama] 43%

  • Per-provider brand icons (from your installed CodexBar app).
  • Weekly usage percent, color-coded: orange under 20% left, red under 10%.
  • Dropped the standalone Gemini entry — Antigravity already covers Gemini.
  • New per-provider toggles in Settings → Advanced → AI Quotas.

Still off by default, still local-only.

Full release notes on GitHub

item.title: 0.11.13-fire.9.6
item.description:
fire.9.6 — AI Quotas shows what's left, not what's used

The menu-bar readout now shows how much of each provider's weekly quota you have remaining, instead of how much you have already used:

  [Codex] 78%  [Claude] 89%  [Antigravity] 100%  [Ollama] 57%

  • A high number now means plenty of headroom; a small red number means you are almost out.
  • Antigravity at full quota reads "100%" instead of a confusing "0%".
  • Color thresholds unchanged: orange under 20% left, red under 10%.
  • Now matches the dropdown, which already lists "X% left" per window.

Still off by default, still local-only.

Full release notes on GitHub
